original text:
Siberian State Aerospace University named after academician M.F.
Reshetnev has a long history.
In 1960 it was founded as a plant and technical college.
This date is marked as the beginning of the development of a new level of education, and changed the lives of many people.
In the past five decades Siberian State Aerospace University has graduated highly qualified specialists in various fields of science and industry.
But what makes Siberian State Aerospace University one of the best institutions in the field of education?
It all starts with the fact that our university includes faculties and institutes in all fields of science.
In spite of the fact that Siberian State Aerospace University is a technical university, it perfectly combines legal, humanitarian and economic spheres.
Moreover, our university is famous for its athletes, singers, artists and humorists.
The spirit in Siberian State Aerospace University is so strong that all teaching staff is like a big family and helps students in everything.
It means that each person can find their second, cosy home there.
So let us congratulate our favourite educational institution on the 55th anniversary!
We wish it further development, creation and implementation of the great scientific projects and, of course, wonderful and smart students!

These moments may not seem much now, but it will definitely appreciate with time.
It's more than just photography, at The Pixel Flair we strive to capture those cherished moments with you.

original text:
The IEA, working as a sub-contractor to Transport Infrastructure Ireland (TII) has been playing an active role in the development and promotion of an Intermodal Freight Route Planner within the EU-EIP intelligent transport systems East-West Corridor project.
The main source of the funding for the project is the European Union's Connecting Europe Facility.
The route planner, while initially focussed on the East-West Corridor running from Ireland through to Central Europe, now covers destinations throughout Europe and further afield.

original text:
We develop an EAM training plan collaboratively with each client.
We typically utilize a dual-track method of training whereby users are trained on the software application as well as on their defined and documented business processes.
We provide classroom and in-the-field training classes as a standard part of the implementation process.
These classes instruct users on everything from basic navigation to work order, inventory, and purchasing processes.
Classroom training focuses on developing and reviewing workflows, data codes, and roles and responsibilities, and software functionality, as well as how to execute in EAM the processes outlined in the client Asset Value Model.
Go-live simulation events are conducted that include both software and business process elements.
In-the-field training is hands-on training in the work environment which applies concepts learned in the classroom and is focused on both process and software elements.
This training is performed with "live" data and occurs during and after EAM system go-live.
This type of training embeds new software tasks and business processes into the client asset management operation.
We also provide "role books" which contain core training material produced during an implementation engagement and include detailed standard operating procedures (SOPs) integrating work processes with application work instructions.
These SOPs are further developed to include client-specific business requirements and application level detail (i.e., screen shots and field notes) as part of the implementation phase.

original text:
417 head of cattle were yarded at the 9th Annual Santa Gertrudis Show & Store Sale at Beaudesert on Saturday 30th March.
With a quality yarding coming to hand, the market improved for all descriptions.
Heifers with breeding potential sold to a very enthusiastic panel of buyers.
The Beutel Family, Warrillee Santa Gertrudis, Bunburra presented a great draft of Santa cattle with their Santa steers 12 months selling to $820.
Col Kleinschmidt, Maudsland sold Santa weaner steers for $725.
Stuart and Katherine Saunders, Boonah sold Euro x weaner steers for $680.
Euro x steers 12 months account William Taylor, Purga sold for $635.
Dean and Tanya Zurvas, Mt Alford sold Euro x weaner steers for $460.
Charolais x weaner steers account Veraart Livestock Enterprises sold for $400.
The Beutel Family sold Santa heifers 12 months for $680.
HC Pastoral Co, Toowoomba sold a line of 80 Droughtmaster heifers 12 months with the top pen selling for $660 to average $618.
Santa weaner heifers account Col Kleinschmidt sold for $595.
Stuart and Katherine Saunders sold Euro x weaner heifers for $640.
Euro x cows and calves account Richard and Anne Worley, Coulson sold for $1,020.
Richard and Anne also sold Droughtmaster cows for $910.
CPZ Farms, Woongoolba sold Murray Grey cows for $690.

original text:
The 2010 African Economic Outlook finds the continent reeling from the effects of the world's deepest and most widespread recession in half a century.
Many countries are at risk of falling far off track to achieving the Millennium Development Goal of halving extreme poverty and hunger by 2015.
Just how hard was the region hit?
What are the prospects for recovery?
Insights and answers in this edition including an in-depth study of Taxation and Aid in Africa.
It was presented in Abidjan, Co虃te d'Ivoire at the 45th African Development Bank Annual Meetings, on May 24.
Now in its ninth year, the AEO is the only report on Africa which applies a common analytical framework to every country, every year.
This allows for a comparison of economic prospects at the regional, sub-regional and country levels.
Produced by the OECD Development Centre, the African Development Bank and the United Nations Economic Commission for Africa, the AEO is the essential reference on Africa.
It benefits from the support of the European Commission.
HOW USEFUL IS THE AFRICAN ECONOMIC OUTLOOK FOR MAURITIUS?
80% of the African countries covered in the AEO registered positive growth in 2009 as compared to only 10% of OECD countries..
Low Income Countries in Africa still collect less than 15% of GDP in taxes while Upper Middle Income countries collect 35%, almost on par with OECD countries.
Africa is one of the most undiversified regions in the world: approximately 80% of its exports are based in oil, minerals and agricultural goods..
Resource-related taxes have increased from 5 to 15% of GDP over the last 15 years.
In Equatorial Guinea alone,over 95% of taxes collected come from natural resources.
HOW TO OBTAIN THE PAPER VERSION OF THIS PUBLICATION?

In order to build a corporate culture and forge bonds with your partners and customers, we always believe that people need to be part of a common event.
The success of any event organized in France lies in the commitment to running the event.
The outcome of an event will be successful if the event planner inculcates professionalism and expertise skills.
The event planner is expected to have a sense of responsibility.
Thus he should use a distinctive, innovative approach that strives to add value to the event.
The budget is an overall determinant of the event quality.
1.
W袧袗孝 孝Y袪袝 袨F 袝V袝螡孝?
An event planner can't organize an event successfully without knowing the type of event.
The event planner needs to know if the corporate event is a meeting, a conference, a business fair or a product launch.
It could equally be an alumni event or a recruitment fair.
Whatever the case, knowing the exact nature of the event will enable the event planner to automate manual tasks.
Such knowledge will also help to estimate the budget thereby facilitating preparations across the various departments.
2.
W袧袗孝 R袝G袉袨螡袇 袧袗袇 Y袨UR 袪R袝F袝R袝螡小袝?
If you want to host an event in France, it is also essential to know the right region.
Paris and Riviera-Monaco are some of the best destinations to host events in France.
The corporate event planner or French DMC still needs to make a decision on where the event will take place.
He may decide that the event will hold at the heart of a prestigious hotel, in a boat or even on the roof of a restaurant.
You should take note that France and most of its big cities have unique event sites.
However, an exceptional opportunity to invite professionals for a meeting or workshop could change the scene.
It is advisable to choose service providers in the city where the event will hold.
For instance, if you settle for Paris, all your service providers will likely come from there too.
It's cheaper this way and it's easy to estimate a budget after choosing the preferred region.
We believe that France is one of the best destinations in the world.
The diversity of cities, landscapes, history and gastronomy are strong motto for a majority of our clients.
3.
W袧袗孝 袇孝YL袝 袨F V袝螡U袝?
France is a multicultural country so selecting a venue style for an event is quite easy.
France offers a range of modern, traditional, and even trendy event styles.
Therefore, the event planner should reach out to vendors and suppliers as soon as he books the venue.
Arranging a kick-off meeting with these people will prevent last minute effort duplications.
Also, last minute arrangements can cost extra if the vendors are forced to adjust their schedules to accommodate your needs.
It is imperative to choose an event style that matches your budget.
You should not opt for a very high style which you can't afford.
Also, ensure that everything is available for the event dates.
Once you make the decision about the style, it will be easy for you to draw its budget.
4.
W袧袗孝 袪袝R袉袨D 袨F 孝袧袝 Y袝袗R D袨 Y袨U 袪L袗螡 孝袧袝 袝V袝螡孝?
The period of the year is another crucial factor to put into considering while estimating the budget for an event in France.
France has warm and cold seasons, and the event manager can advise which period is suitable for a particular event.
If you are planning an event in Paris, it's better to do it between June and October.
The reason is that this period is relatively warm and your guests will be comfortable.
Also, summer activities are quite different from winter activities.
Therefore different seasons will probably have different costs.
Each period has his key features.
The local Dmc is your best adviser to select the appropriate destination regarding the period of the year.
5.
袧袨W L袨螡G 袇袧袨ULD 孝袧袝 袝V袝螡孝 袙袝?
The duration of the event is a crucial factor to consider when planning an event in France.
The length of the event will help the event organizer to better plan for the event.
Knowledge of the duration will also guide you in drawing a practical budget for the event.
It is advisable for the French DMC to check out affordable venues and adjust dates to minimize cost.
More so, the event planner can play with the event date.
For example, you could start the conference in the afternoon so that you don't spend on breakfast and lunch.
6.
D袨 Y袨U R袝QU袉R袝 袗小小袨袦袨D袗孝袉袨螡?
Accommodation is another important consideration to make when drawing a budget for an event in France.
You need to decide whether or not you will provide accommodation for your guests.
You might want to minimize cost by opting for twin bedrooms in the hotel.
The advantage is that two attendees get to share a single room.
Whatever the case, just have in mind that many hotels in France offer very competitive and attractive group rates.
The majority of corporates go for 4 star properties.
You will find a larger choice and the best deals for groups in this category.
7.
袧袨W 袦袗螡Y 袪袝袨袪L袝 孝袨 袙UDG袝孝?
Knowledge of the number of people you are expecting will help you come up with a reasonable budget.
If you are expecting many people, you can try to negotiate a package price for certain services.
For instance, when it comes to transporting your guests, you may ask to be billed per bus instead of per person.
You might also opt for vans instead of buses.
Such attitudes could help you reduce expenditures.
The event planner should also be able to compare the costs per person and the expenditures per groups of attendees.
8.
D袨 Y袨U REQUIRE 袗 袇袉孝袝 V袉袇袉孝?
袗 s褨t械 褨ns褉械褋t褨芯n 褨s th械 b械st w邪褍 t芯 m邪k械 褍芯ur f褨n邪l d械褋褨s褨芯n.

W械 kn芯w th邪t 褍芯u w邪nt th械 b械st 械v械nt 芯ut th械r械, but th械 r械邪l褨t褍 褨s th邪t n芯t 械v械r褍芯n械 褨s 械褏褉械褋t褨ng 邪 m邪st械r褉褨械褋械.
袇芯m械t褨m械s th械 械v械nt 褬ust h邪s t芯 w芯rk.

original text:
Today I want to share with you all the lovely 'Oakwood Soaperie', a bath and body care company which I came across this week, whilst I was attending a Summer fair at Brancepeth castle.
Oakwood Soaperie was established in 2010 by Ceri Aitman (who also makes all of the products which are available!)
and is based in the heart of the North East countryside in the Gibside Estate.
They have a wide range of super lovely, luxurious soap bars infused with natural plant fragrances and butters, bath oils and salts, as well as bath bombs and bubble bars to take your bath from relaxing, to sensationally relaxing and luxuriously indulgent!
All of the products come wrapped in lovely recycled, handmade or waxed paper and adorned with ribbons and ingredients labels, which to me is a lovely finishing touch which makes all the difference.
If you are looking for a nice gift for a friend/relative and are unsure what to get, why not check out Ceri's website here and get the prezzies delivered to your door!
The soaps come in a wide array of fragrances, from 'Mint and Seed' and 'Cinnamon Fig', to 'Restful Slumber' which aids a good night's sleep!
The soaps look and smell AMAZING, which is all down to the use of tip-top ingredients and Ceri's curing process which lasts between 4-6 weeks, to ensure the soaps are mild and kind to sensitive skin.
Another thing which I really like about Oakwood Soaperie is that it is an animal testing free zone, and many of the products are also vegan friendly, which makes it ethically good for everyone!
I don't know about you, but I had never heard of solid body butter bars until I saw them here and they sound great, ideal for holiday as it is small and compact, and will not leak either!
Win, win!
I bought a Northumbrian Honey, goat's milk and oat bath bomb which is ideal for sensitive skin.
If you'd like to follow Oakwood Soaperie, you can on Facebook, Twitter, Pinterest and Instagram!
There is even a blog!
Be sure to check our Ceri's online shop - you might just find the perfect gift or treat for yourself!

This is especially for APU students who wish to conduct job hunting activities in the Kanto and Kansai areas.
Ritsumeikan affiliated universities offer various support for your job hunting activities, but please be advised to contact each university directly for specific information.
Each university runs on different schedules where long holidays and exam periods may differ.
Their counseling hours and other services will change accordingly.
Please check the respective homepages or you can also contact them by phone for further details.
Mainly Provided Assistance Employment counseling (check entry sheet, practice for interviews), issue necessary certificates (only for current students), changing room facilities, luggage storing, computer facilities and also resume format will be sold.
Mainly Provided Assistance Employment counseling (Job hunting counseling in general, check entry sheet), issue necessary certificates (only for current students), employment related newspapers and other official announcements will be displayed, computer facilities, photocopy service (cost borne by students) and also resume format will be sold.
Mainly Provided Assistance Employment counseling (check entry sheet, practice for interviews), issue necessary certificates (only for current students) and computer facilities.
